_source: ASQ Quality standards, Six Sigma methodology, ISO 9001 quality management, Statistical process control references
_note: Long-tail quality control knowledge. Inspection procedures, SPC charts, Six Sigma DMAIC, measurement systems, ISO standards, quality tools.

incoming_inspection.sampling_plans: AQL (Acceptable Quality Limit); ANSI/ASQ Z1.4 standard; sample size based on lot size and inspection level; accept/reject criteria; example: Lot 1000 pcs, General Inspection Level II, AQL 1.0% = sample 80 pcs, accept if ≤2 defects, reject if ≥3
incoming_inspection.receiving_inspection: Verify quantity against packing slip; check for shipping damage; measure critical dimensions; test functionality (if applicable); compare to specification sheet; quarantine until approved
incoming_inspection.certificate_of_conformance: Supplier certifies material meets specs; reduces inspection burden for trusted suppliers; verify periodically (skip lot inspection); maintain records
incoming_inspection.supplier_quality: Approved vendor list; audit suppliers; performance metrics (defect rate, on-time delivery); corrective action for issues; develop partnerships

in_process_inspection.first_article_inspection: FAI; inspect first piece after setup; verify all dimensions, features, specifications before production run; prevents batch of defects; AS9102 in aerospace
in_process_inspection.patrol_inspection: Periodic checks during production; every hour, every 50 pieces, etc.; catch process drift before many defects made; plot on control charts
in_process_inspection.operator_self_inspection: Operator checks own work; immediate feedback; reduces defects passed to next operation; requires training and trust
in_process_inspection.critical_characteristics: Key dimensions/features affecting fit, function, safety; 100% inspection or high-frequency sampling; SPC monitoring; may use automated inspection (vision systems, CMM)

final_inspection.100_percent_inspection: Every unit inspected; critical safety items, low-volume high-value, customer requirement; expensive but ensures quality
final_inspection.sampling_inspection: Statistical sample represents lot; faster, cheaper than 100%; risk of accepting bad lot or rejecting good lot (producer/consumer risk); use when destructive testing or high volume
final_inspection.functional_testing: Operate product to verify performance; leak test, pressure test, electrical test, cycle test; simulate end-use conditions; record data
final_inspection.cosmetic_inspection: Appearance; scratches, dents, color, finish; often subjective; use visual standards (limit samples showing acceptable vs defect); lighting critical

measurement_tools.micrometers: 0.001" (0.01mm) accuracy; outside mic (OD), inside mic (ID), depth mic; anvil faces parallel; calibrate regularly; zero before use
measurement_tools.calipers: 0.001" (0.02mm digital, 0.001" vernier) accuracy; OD, ID, depth, step; digital easier to read; battery life issue; vernier mechanical backup
measurement_tools.height_gage: Measure height from surface plate; 0.001" accuracy; scribing, layout, comparative measurement; granite surface plate (Grade A ±0.00005"/ft)
measurement_tools.cmm: Coordinate Measuring Machine; 0.0001" accuracy; 3D measurement; CNC probing; expensive ($50k-$500k+); climate-controlled room; aerospace, automotive
measurement_tools.optical_comparator: Project magnified shadow; overlay tolerance chart; 10-50× magnification; 2D profile inspection; threads, gears, complex shapes
measurement_tools.gage_blocks: Precision reference standards; 0.00002" accuracy (Grade 0); calibrate other tools; set fixtures; expensive, handle carefully

control_charts.xbar_r_chart: Variable data (measurements); X-bar (average) and R (range) charts; subgroup size 2-10; monitors process center and variation; most common
control_charts.individuals_chart: X and mR (moving range); subgroup size 1; slow processes, destructive testing, expensive measurements
control_charts.p_chart: Attribute data (pass/fail); proportion defective; variable sample size; example: % defective parts per shift
control_charts.c_chart: Count of defects; constant sample size; example: number of surface defects per panel
control_charts.control_limits: UCL (Upper Control Limit) = X-bar + A2×R-bar; LCL = X-bar - A2×R-bar; calculated from data (not specifications); ±3 sigma from mean; 99.73% of data within if in control

interpreting_charts.out_of_control_signals: Point beyond control limit (immediate action); 7+ consecutive points above or below center line (trend); 7+ consecutive points increasing or decreasing (run); 2 of 3 points >2σ from center (Zone A); 4 of 5 points >1σ (Zone B); 15 points in Zone C (hugging center line, stratification)
interpreting_charts.common_cause_variation: Natural process variation; random; within control limits; stable over time; reduce only by process improvement (not adjustments)
interpreting_charts.special_cause_variation: Assignable cause; out-of-control signal; investigate and eliminate (tool wear, material change, operator error, machine malfunction); adjust process
interpreting_charts.overadjustment: Tampering; adjusting process in control; increases variation; react only to special causes; resist urge to tweak stable process

process_capability.cp: Process Capability; Cp = (USL - LSL) / 6σ; measures potential capability (centered process); Cp ≥1.33 (4σ) acceptable, ≥1.67 (5σ) good, ≥2.0 (6σ) excellent
process_capability.cpk: Process Capability Index; Cpk = min[(USL - μ) / 3σ, (μ - LSL) / 3σ]; accounts for centering; Cpk <1.0 = producing defects, 1.0-1.33 marginal, >1.33 acceptable
process_capability.ppk: Process Performance Index; uses actual process σ (not subgroup σ like Cpk); long-term capability; accounts for all variation sources
process_capability.interpretation: Cp vs Cpk: if equal, process centered; if Cpk << Cp, process off-center (adjust mean); if both low, reduce variation (Six Sigma project)

sampling.random_sampling: Every unit equal chance; prevents bias; use random number generator or systematic sampling (every nth piece); don't cherry-pick
sampling.sample_size: Larger sample = more confidence, more expensive; n=30 minimum for normal distribution assumption; calculator or tables for proportion/mean testing
sampling.confidence_interval: Range likely containing true population parameter; 95% confidence typical; sample mean ± (t-value × s / √n); narrows with larger n
sampling.hypothesis_testing: Null hypothesis (no difference); alternative (there is difference); p-value <0.05 reject null (95% confidence); Type I error (false positive), Type II (false negative)

dmaic.define: Project charter; problem statement; goal (reduce defects 50%); scope; stakeholders; VOC (Voice of Customer); CTQ (Critical to Quality) tree; high-level process map (SIPOC - Suppliers, Inputs, Process, Outputs, Customers)
dmaic.measure: Current state baseline; select CTQ metrics; operational definition (exact measurement method); validate measurement system (Gage R&R); collect data; process capability baseline (current Cpk, defect rate)
dmaic.analyze: Root cause analysis; Pareto chart (80/20 rule - vital few vs trivial many); fishbone diagram (Ishikawa - 6M: Man, Machine, Material, Method, Measurement, Mother Nature); hypothesis testing; regression analysis; identify variables affecting CTQ
dmaic.improve: Brainstorm solutions (address root causes); pilot test; DOE (Design of Experiments) to optimize; implement improvements; mistake-proofing (poka-yoke); update procedures; train operators
dmaic.control: Control plan; SPC charts; audits; standard work; sustain gains; hand off to process owner; monitor KPIs; celebrate success; document lessons learned

six_sigma_metrics.dpmo: Defects Per Million Opportunities; (Defects / (Units × Opportunities)) × 1,000,000; industry-standard metric; 6σ = 3.4 DPMO
six_sigma_metrics.yield: First Pass Yield (FPY); % of units passing without rework; Rolled Throughput Yield (RTY) = FPY1 × FPY2 × FPY3...; accounts for cumulative defects across process steps
six_sigma_metrics.cost_of_poor_quality: COPQ; scrap, rework, warranty, inspection, customer complaints; typically 15-25% of revenue; Six Sigma reduces to <10%

quality_tools.pareto_chart: Bar chart + cumulative line; prioritize (80% of problems from 20% of causes); focus improvement efforts on vital few
quality_tools.fishbone_diagram: Cause-and-effect; brainstorm all potential causes; organize by categories (6M); drill down to root cause
quality_tools.5_whys: Ask 'why' 5 times to drill to root cause; simple but effective; example: Why is machine down? Bearing failed. Why? No lubrication. Why? Missed PM. Why? No schedule. Why? No PM program. Root cause: implement PM program
quality_tools.fmea: Failure Modes and Effects Analysis; proactive; identify potential failures; Severity × Occurrence × Detection = RPN (Risk Priority Number); prioritize mitigation actions for high RPN
quality_tools.control_plan: Document critical parameters, measurement method, sample size, frequency, reaction plan; living document; updated with process changes

design_of_experiments.purpose: Optimize process; understand variable interactions; efficient (fewer trials than one-factor-at-a-time); statistical rigor
design_of_experiments.full_factorial: All combinations of all levels; 2^k experiments (k factors, 2 levels each); example: 3 factors = 2^3 = 8 runs; identifies main effects and interactions
design_of_experiments.fractional_factorial: Subset of full factorial; fewer runs; assumes some interactions negligible; screening experiments (many factors, identify significant few)
design_of_experiments.response_surface: Optimize settings; Box-Behnken, Central Composite designs; curved response surface; find optimal sweet spot
design_of_experiments.analysis: ANOVA (Analysis of Variance); identifies significant factors; regression equation predicts response; validate with confirmation runs

iso_9001.principles: Customer focus, leadership, engagement of people, process approach, improvement, evidence-based decisions, relationship management
iso_9001.requirements: Context of organization, leadership, planning, support, operation, performance evaluation, improvement; documented procedures; management review; internal audits; corrective action
iso_9001.certification: Third-party audit by registrar; initial audit (documentation review + on-site), surveillance audits (annually), recertification (every 3 years); maintains customer confidence; some industries require
iso_9001.benefits: Standardized processes, continuous improvement culture, customer satisfaction, reduced waste, employee engagement, competitive advantage (required by some customers)

as9100.aerospace_quality: Based on ISO 9001 + aerospace-specific requirements; configuration management, risk management, FOD (Foreign Object Debris) prevention, counterfeit parts prevention
as9100.nadcap: National Aerospace and Defense Contractors Accreditation Program; special processes (heat treating, welding, NDT, chemical processing); strict auditing; costly but required for aerospace suppliers

iatf_16949.automotive_quality: Based on ISO 9001 + automotive requirements (GM, Ford, Chrysler, VW, etc.); PPAP (Production Part Approval Process), APQP (Advanced Product Quality Planning), control plans, MSA (Measurement Systems Analysis)
iatf_16949.core_tools: APQP, PPAP, FMEA, MSA, SPC; required by automotive OEMs; zero-defect mentality; continuous improvement (Kaizen)

document_control.procedures: SOPs (Standard Operating Procedures); work instructions; who, what, when, how; revision control; approval signatures; distribution to point of use
document_control.records: Objective evidence; inspection records, calibration certificates, training records, audit reports; retention (typically 3-10 years, some forever); traceability
document_control.change_control: ECN (Engineering Change Notice); document changes; approve before implementation; update drawings, procedures, training; notification to affected parties; revision history

gage_r_and_r.purpose: Validate measurement system; separate measurement variation from process variation; ensure gage capable before process capability study
gage_r_and_r.method: 10 parts × 3 operators × 2-3 trials = 60-90 measurements; calculate repeatability (equipment variation, EV) and reproducibility (appraiser variation, AV); %GRR = (GRR / Total Variation) × 100
gage_r_and_r.acceptance_criteria: %GRR <10% excellent, 10-30% acceptable (may be OK depending on application), >30% unacceptable (improve measurement system before using for decisions)
gage_r_and_r.improvement: High repeatability (EV) = gage needs calibration, repair, replacement; High reproducibility (AV) = training needed, procedure unclear, gage difficult to use

calibration.frequency: Based on usage, environment, criticality; micrometers annually, thermometers quarterly, load cells monthly; more frequent if critical or harsh environment
calibration.traceability: NIST (National Institute of Standards and Technology) traceable; standards calibrated against higher-level standards, ultimately to NIST; unbroken chain; certificate required
calibration.out_of_tolerance: If found OOT during calibration, review all measurements since last calibration; assess impact; may need to recall product, notify customers, corrective action
calibration.calibration_labels: Sticker on gage; calibration date, due date, calibration lab, unique ID; do not use if expired; quarantine until recalibrated

attribute_agreement.purpose: Validate attribute inspection (pass/fail, visual); measure inspector consistency and accuracy
attribute_agreement.method: 30-50 samples (mix of good and bad, known truth); 2-3 inspectors; 2 trials; calculate: inspector vs self (within-appraiser), inspector vs standard (vs truth), inspector vs inspector (between-appraiser)
attribute_agreement.acceptance: >90% agreement with self and standard; >80% between appraisers; if low, improve standards (limit samples), training, lighting, magnification

8d_problem_solving.d1_team: Assemble cross-functional team; relevant expertise; empowered to implement solutions
8d_problem_solving.d2_describe: Problem description; 5W2H (Who, What, When, Where, Why, How, How many); contain problem (interim action to prevent spreading)
8d_problem_solving.d3_containment: Immediate actions to protect customer; sort and inspect inventory, 100% inspection, rework, recall; buy time for permanent fix
8d_problem_solving.d4_root_cause: 5 Whys, fishbone, fault tree analysis; verify root cause (recreate problem by reintroducing cause, eliminate by removing cause)
8d_problem_solving.d5_permanent_corrective_action: Develop solutions; pilot test; verify effective; implement; poka-yoke (mistake-proof) preferred
8d_problem_solving.d6_implement: Roll out to production; update procedures, train, verify effectiveness over time
8d_problem_solving.d7_prevent_recurrence: Systemic improvements; update FMEA, control plan, work instructions; apply lessons learned to similar processes
8d_problem_solving.d8_team_recognition: Celebrate success; recognize team contributions; document lessons learned; close report

is_is_not_analysis.structure: IS: What is the problem? When/where does it occur? How much? | IS NOT: What related items are OK? When/where does it NOT occur?
is_is_not_analysis.distinctions: Differences between IS and IS NOT columns; clues to root cause; example: Problem on Line A (IS) but not Line B (IS NOT) → investigate differences between lines
is_is_not_analysis.focus: Narrows investigation; what's unique about the problem conditions; what changed?

cost_categories.prevention: Training, process improvement, quality planning, preventive maintenance, supplier development; proactive; 5-10% of COPQ; high ROI
cost_categories.appraisal: Inspection, testing, audits, calibration, gage R&R; finding defects; 10-20% of COPQ; necessary but non-value-added
cost_categories.internal_failure: Scrap, rework, re-inspection, downtime, engineering changes; caught before customer; 25-40% of COPQ; waste
cost_categories.external_failure: Warranty, returns, recalls, liability, lost sales, reputation damage; reached customer; 25-40% of COPQ; most expensive
cost_categories.goal: Shift spending to prevention (lowest total COPQ); reduce appraisal and failure costs; optimal quality cost ~2-4% of sales (world-class)

hidden_factory.concept: Rework, sorting, expediting, firefighting; parallel operation fixing defects; hides true capacity; increases lead time
hidden_factory.impact: Rework = 10-20% of production capacity; eliminate to increase throughput without capital investment; measure OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) to reveal hidden losses

kaizen.philosophy: Small, incremental improvements; everyone involved; daily improvement culture; Japanese: 'change for better'
kaizen.kaizen_event: Blitz; focused 3-5 day improvement workshop; cross-functional team; specific problem; rapid implementation; celebrate quick wins
kaizen.pdca_cycle: Plan-Do-Check-Act (Deming); Plan (identify improvement, plan change), Do (test on small scale), Check (analyze results), Act (implement if successful or adjust and repeat)
kaizen.suggestion_system: Employees submit improvement ideas; review and implement; recognition/rewards; engagement and ownership; Toyota average 50+ suggestions per employee per year

lean_integration.waste_elimination: 8 wastes (DOWNTIME): Defects, Overproduction, Waiting, Not utilizing talent, Transportation, Inventory, Motion, Extra processing; quality defects = prime waste
lean_integration.value_stream_mapping: Map current state (all steps, value-added vs non-value-added); design future state (eliminate waste); action plan to close gap
lean_integration.5s: Sort (remove unnecessary), Set in order (organize), Shine (clean), Standardize (procedures), Sustain (discipline); foundation for quality; visual management
lean_integration.jidoka: Automation with human touch; machines stop at abnormality; separate human work from machine work; prevent defects from progressing; andon (visual alert)

benchmarking.internal: Compare across plants, lines, shifts; best practices sharing; easiest to access data
benchmarking.competitive: Compare to direct competitors; market position; difficult to get data; industry surveys, trade shows
benchmarking.functional: Compare to best-in-class (any industry); breakthrough ideas; example: pit crew process (NASCAR) applied to hospital ER patient flow
benchmarking.process: Site visits, interviews, data collection, gap analysis, adapt best practices (don't copy blindly - fit to culture)

design_for_quality.dfss: Design for Six Sigma; DMADV (Define, Measure, Analyze, Design, Verify); proactive quality in design phase; prevents defects vs detecting
design_for_quality.qfd: Quality Function Deployment; House of Quality matrix; customer requirements → engineering characteristics → design targets; prioritize features; competitive analysis
design_for_quality.robust_design: Taguchi methods; design insensitive to noise (environmental variation, component variation); parameter design (optimal settings); tolerance design (minimize variation)

non_destructive_testing.visual: Simplest; naked eye or magnified; surface defects; cracks, corrosion, misalignment; borescope for internal inspection
non_destructive_testing.dye_penetrant: Liquid penetrant seeps into surface cracks; developer draws it out; visible or fluorescent dye; finds small surface cracks
non_destructive_testing.magnetic_particle: Ferromagnetic materials; magnetic field applied; particles accumulate at cracks; surface and near-surface defects
non_destructive_testing.ultrasonic: Sound waves; pulse-echo or through-transmission; internal defects, thickness measurement; welds, castings, composites; requires couplant
non_destructive_testing.radiography: X-ray or gamma ray; film or digital detector; internal defects; welds, castings; 2D image of 3D part; safety concerns (radiation)
non_destructive_testing.eddy_current: Conductive materials; induced currents; surface and near-surface cracks, coating thickness, conductivity; fast, no couplant

reliability_engineering.mtbf: Mean Time Between Failures; 1 / failure rate; repairable systems; higher is better; calculated from field data or predicted from component MTBFs
reliability_engineering.weibull_analysis: Failure distribution; shape parameter β (β<1 infant mortality, β=1 random, β>1 wear-out); scale parameter η (characteristic life); predict warranty costs, optimize maintenance
reliability_engineering.reliability_testing: ALT (Accelerated Life Testing); overstress to induce failures quickly; model degradation; predict field life; save time vs real-time testing
reliability_engineering.halt_hass: Highly Accelerated Life Test (design phase, find weaknesses) / Highly Accelerated Stress Screen (production, screen out infant mortality); thermal cycling, vibration, voltage variation
